_meta.description: Tracking artifact for Geometric Algebra exploration of Observer-Centrism derivations
_meta.assessmentNotes: Post-upgrade assessment: all 11 candidates upgraded from stub to provisional. Electromagnetism, lorentz-invariance, spin-statistics, and cpt-theorem upgraded to rigorous (Rounds 1-4). Working-note comments fixed across spin-statistics, cpt-theorem, weak-interaction, chirality-selection. Sign error fixed in lorentz-invariance Proposition 4.1 proof. Sign error fixed in spin-statistics Theorem 3.1 kernel proof. Logical inconsistency fixed in spin-statistics Theorem 5.2. Round 4 (cpt-theorem): fixed e₁₂₃²=−1→+1 error in Definition 2.1, extra minus in Proposition 3.1 PT formula, two working-note self-corrections (Propositions 2.2 and 4.2), sloppy intermediate step in Proposition 1.2. Round 5 (chirality-selection): fixed self-dual eigenvalue convention (IB=+iB not -iB for self-dual), swapped L/R labels on chirality conditions in Prop 3.2, fixed Definition 1.2 projectors (stated correct form upfront), tightened Prop 6.2 proof with centrality argument and explicit N_k^+ P_R = 0 computation, fixed Hodge dual sign in Remark after Prop 2.2.

candidates.title: Lorentz Group via STA Rotors
candidates.rationale: STA was designed for this. Rotors unify boosts and rotations into a single algebraic object — both are elements of the even subalgebra differing only in the grade-2 element used.

genuineInsights: Boosts and rotations unified as exp(-Bθ/2) — only the bivector B differs (timelike vs spacelike)
Thomas precession automatic: composition of boosts e₀₂ and e₀₁ contains spatial rotation e₁₂ as algebraic necessity
Double cover intrinsic: R and -R give same transformation, no need for separate SU(2) construction
Loop closure parallel: rotor periodicity R(4π) = +1 mirrors the observer loop axiom
limitations: Time dilation and length contraction derivations not computationally shorter
Physical predictions identical — no new testable content
assessment.additionalPromise: Foundational for 6+ other GA pages. Best candidate for rigorous upgrade — all results are standard STA (Hestenes, Doran-Lasenby). Step 9 (Poincaré group via translations) could connect to gauge theory gravity. The rotor periodicity ↔ loop closure parallel is unique to this framework.

candidates.title: Maxwell Equations as ∇F = J
candidates.rationale: The electromagnetic field F = E + IB is a bivector in STA. Maxwell's four equations compress to a single equation ∇F = J. Charge quantization maps to quantized bivector rotations.

genuineInsights: Four Maxwell equations → one: ∇F = J, with ∇·F = J (grade-1) and ∇∧F = 0 (grade-3) automatic
Charge conservation as grade consistency: ∇·J = 0 follows from ∇²F having no grade-0 part
Uniqueness argument in one line: ∇F = J with F = ∇∧A forces Maxwell structure
Electromagnetic duality = multiplication by pseudoscalar I: F → IF swaps E and B
Magnetic monopole exclusion from gauge structure: F = ∇∧A forces ∇∧F = 0
limitations: No new physics beyond standard Maxwell theory
Coupling constant e still requires separate derivation
Wave solutions not computationally shorter
assessment.additionalPromise: Most pedagogically powerful GA page. The ∇F = J compression is the single most compelling GA result. Could serve as the showcase example for the overview page. All results are textbook STA — very ready for rigorous upgrade.

candidates.title: CPT as a Single Cl(1,3) Object
candidates.rationale: P, T, C map to specific Cl(1,3) operations. CPT becomes a single algebraic element (the pseudoscalar I = e₀₁₂₃) whose commutativity with even-grade elements is immediate.

genuineInsights: CPT = pseudoscalar I = e₀₁₂₃: the combined operation is a single algebraic object
CPT invariance = I commutes with all even-grade physics (one line proof)
Violation pattern: P(I) = -I in odd dimensions → parity violation is the pseudoscalar being odd-grade
Pseudoscalar's triple role unified: oriented volume element, PT operator, and chirality operator are the same object
Spin-statistics-CPT connection via even subalgebra: CPT preserving Cl⁺(1,3) links to spin-statistics
limitations: Charge conjugation (C) construction not simpler than standard approach
Full Jost-Lüders-Pauli theorem still needed for rigorous completeness
Individual C, P, T violations require additional structure beyond I
assessment.additionalPromise: The pseudoscalar as CPT is a genuine conceptual unification. The connection to chirality-selection (same object I appears in both) creates the strongest cross-page link in the GA section. Could yield a unified CPT-chirality page at rigorous level.

candidates.title: Chirality as Grade Structure
candidates.rationale: GA makes chirality explicit through grade structure. Left/right projectors (1 ± γ₅)/2 become (1 ± I)/2 where I is the pseudoscalar. Chirality selection is grade-projection selection.

genuineInsights: Chirality from one object: the pseudoscalar I defines the chiral split
Parity violation in one line: P(I) = -I, so parity maps left ↔ right projectors
Mass-chirality connection: Dirac mass term mixes left and right = mixes I eigenspaces
Grade structure pattern across gauge hierarchy: U(1) grade-0, SU(2) grade-1 (bivectors), SU(3) grade-2
d = 3+1 minimality: the only dimension where I² = -1 (needed for projectors) AND non-abelian gauge groups exist
limitations: Quaternionic orientation argument from target derivation still needed
Why left and not right remains a convention/boundary condition choice
No computational simplification of chirality anomaly
assessment.additionalPromise: The d = 3+1 minimality result (Step 8) is potentially the most framework-relevant GA insight: it provides a dimensional selection argument from Clifford algebra structure alone. Combined with CPT page (same pseudoscalar), these two pages together make a strong case for GA's structural value. The grade pattern across the gauge hierarchy deserves deeper exploration.

candidates.title: Cayley-Dickson vs Clifford Relationship
candidates.rationale: GA explains why the associativity ceiling terminates at O. The isomorphism C⊗O ≅ Cl(6) is already referenced in anomaly-cancellation and standard-model-group. The Cayley-Dickson construction parallels Clifford doubling but diverges at step 4.

genuineInsights: Associativity relocation: octonion non-associativity → grade-3 associator elements of Cl(6), making it tractable
C⊗O ≅ Cl(6) with physical motivation for complexification (quantum mechanics requires C)
Fermion quantum numbers emerge from Cl(6) grading structure
Dual termination mechanisms: Cayley-Dickson loses associativity, Clifford loses division — same wall, two perspectives
Partial resolution of bootstrap Gap 4: Clifford associativity explains why the gauge hierarchy terminates
limitations: SU(2)_L embedding in Cl(6) not as clean as SU(3) embedding
Three generations still unexplained by Cl(6) alone
Full Clifford-to-Standard-Model functor not constructed
assessment.additionalPromise: The most structurally novel page — it directly addresses an open gap in the core derivation chain (bootstrap Gap 4). The associativity relocation idea (non-associativity becomes grade-3 structure in an associative algebra) is a genuine mathematical insight that could strengthen the bootstrap derivation itself. This page has the highest potential to feed back into the main derivation chain.

existingGAReferences: gauge/standard-model-group (line 136): Cl(6) has natural U(1) × SU(3) action
interactions/bootstrap-division-algebras (line 215): Open gap on Clifford algebras

candidates.title: Spin-Statistics via Cl(3,0) Rotors
candidates.rationale: SU(2)/SO(3) double cover is transparent via Cl(3,0) rotors. The existing derivation uses π₁(SO(3)) = ℤ₂ and SU(2) representation theory; GA makes rotors and the double cover algebraically explicit.

genuineInsights: SU(2) is built into Cl⁺(3,0): no separate construction needed
2π sign flip in one line: exp(-Bπ) = -1 for any unit bivector B
Particle exchange is B² = -1: the exchange operator squares to -1 for half-integer spin
Single-sided vs double-sided rotor action distinguishes spinors from vectors
Dimension dependence from bivector counting: n(n-1)/2 bivectors in Cl(n,0)
Spinors as minimal left ideals — no matrix representation needed
limitations: Topological argument (π₁(SO(3)) = ℤ₂) still required for the connection to statistics
Structural postulate S1 unchanged by GA treatment
Berry phase argument not computationally simpler
assessment.additionalPromise: Strong pedagogical value. The spinors-as-ideals construction is well-established in GA literature. Ready for rigorous upgrade — all results are standard Clifford algebra theory. Foundation for entanglement page.

candidates.title: Weak Interaction via H ≅ Cl⁺(3,0)
candidates.rationale: The quaternion algebra H is isomorphic to Cl⁺(3,0). The three imaginary units I, J, K correspond to bivectors e₂₃, e₃₁, e₁₂. SU(2) gauge transformations are rotor transformations in the even subalgebra.

genuineInsights: H ≅ Cl⁺(3,0) makes quaternions concrete: {i,j,k} → {e₂₃, e₃₁, e₁₂}
Gauge transformations are rotor conjugation: ψ → RψR̃
Self-interaction from one product: the geometric product of different bivectors is non-commutative, forcing non-abelian structure
Bootstrap doubling = adding a dimension: R → C → H corresponds to Cl(0) → Cl(1) → Cl⁺(3,0)
Weak interaction and spin share the same algebra — deep structural connection
limitations: Localization argument (S1 → global → local gauge) unchanged
Yang-Mills uniqueness argument unchanged
Parity violation still requires chirality argument (separate page)
assessment.additionalPromise: The doubling-as-dimension-extension insight connects to division-algebras page. The shared-algebra observation (weak = spin) deserves deeper exploration — could this constrain the weak coupling? Moderate promise for framework feedback. Rigorous upgrade feasible with standard results.

candidates.title: Gravity in STA: Gauge Theory Gravity
candidates.rationale: Gauge theory gravity (Lasenby, Doran, Gull) reformulates GR in flat-space STA with position and rotation gauge fields. Geodesics become rotor equations.

genuineInsights: Geodesic equation simplification: second-order → first-order rotor equation dR/dτ = ½Ω(v)R
Curvature as bivector-valued function R(B) — linear map on bivectors, not rank-4 tensor
Equivalence principle = gauge invariance: position gauge (diffeomorphisms) + rotation gauge (local Lorentz)
Gravity as gauge theory on flat STA: no curved manifold needed, matches framework ontology
Natural horizon regularity: STA is flat, so horizon is a coordinate feature, not a singularity of the algebra
Clean separation of metric (h-function) and connection (Ω-function)
limitations: Schwarzschild solution computation not shorter than standard
Gravitational constant G still empirical, not derived
GTG formalism has known subtleties (global topology recovery)
assessment.additionalPromise: The flat-background ontology strongly aligns with the framework's causal-set-on-Minkowski picture. The GTG formalism is mature (Doran-Lasenby 2003) and well-suited for rigorous treatment. Foundation for einstein-equations and er-epr pages.

candidates.title: Einstein Equations in STA Form
candidates.rationale: The Riemann tensor as a bivector-to-bivector mapping; Einstein equations as G(a) = κT(a) where G and T are linear functions on vectors. Lovelock uniqueness restated in GA language.

genuineInsights: Complete index elimination: Riemann tensor R(B), Ricci R(a), scalar R are all functions, not index arrays
Riemann as linear map on bivectors: R: ⋀²→⋀² makes symmetries obvious
Bianchi identity from one operation: ∂_a ∧ R(a∧b) = 0
Curvature decomposition (Weyl + Ricci + scalar) is linear algebra on bivector space
Fixed-point loop: rotor self-consistency equation (R determines Ω determines R) mirrors coherence loop
limitations: Lovelock theorem same complexity as standard proof
Newtonian limit derivation equivalent effort
No computational advantage for solution-finding
assessment.additionalPromise: Lowest independent value of the STA pages — mostly notational improvement over standard GR. However, the fixed-point loop insight (Step 8) connecting to the framework's coherence loop is genuinely novel and framework-specific. Best treated as a companion to the gravity page rather than standalone.

candidates.title: Entanglement via Rotor Pairs
candidates.rationale: Entangled states correspond to non-factorizable elements in the product algebra. No-cloning follows from GA product rules. Entanglement monogamy maps to bivector sharing constraints.

genuineInsights: Bell states as explicit Clifford product-ideal elements: concrete algebraic objects, not abstract kets
Spin correlations from geometric product: E(â,b̂) = -â·b̂ as scalar part of rotor product
Non-factorizability is algebraic rank: entanglement = rank > 1 in product algebra
Partial reversion replaces partial trace: grade-selective reversion on one factor
Monogamy as finite bivector budget: 3 bivectors in Cl(3,0) constrains sharing
No-cloning from linearity vs quadraticity: cloning would need f(αψ) = αf(ψ) but clone map is quadratic
limitations: No new entropy measure: Shannon-Khinchin axioms force von Neumann form regardless of algebra
No-cloning proof not simpler than standard (linearity argument is the same)
Product algebra formalism less common in GA literature — fewer established results to draw on
CHSH/Tsirelson bounds same derivation effort
assessment.additionalPromise: The bivector budget picture for monogamy is the most original contribution. Could connect to the ER=EPR page (shared bivectors = shared geometry). The product algebra formalism is less developed in the GA literature, so there's room for genuinely new mathematical results, but also more risk of unstable foundations.

candidates.title: ER=EPR via STA Geometry
candidates.rationale: STA provides natural geometric language for wormhole throat geometry. The duality between EPR (bivector entanglement) and ER (rotor-generated curvature) becomes transparent in Cl(1,3).

genuineInsights: Wormholes on flat background: GTG places ER bridges in flat STA, avoiding manifold topology change
Throat as minimal bivector surface: area = ∫|h̲⁻¹(dS)| with orientation from bivector direction
Horizon from position gauge: (h̲⁻¹(e₀))² = 0 at r = 2M — coordinate time becomes null
Grade-2 unification: both entanglement (Cl(3,0)² bivectors) and curvature (Cl(1,3) bivectors) are grade-2 phenomena
Surface gravity from rotation gauge: κ = lim[r→2M] |Ω(e₀)·(e₀∧eᵣ)| gives Hawking temperature
limitations: Two distinct algebras (Cl(1,3) for spacetime, Cl(3,0)² for entanglement) — not unified into one
Schmidt ↔ quasi-normal mode correspondence (Row 5 of Theorem 5.1) remains conjectural
No new predictions beyond the target derivation
assessment.additionalPromise: The bivector-grade correspondence (entanglement and curvature share grade structure) is suggestive but not a proof. The two-algebra problem is the central unsolved challenge. If Cl(1,3) ⊗ Cl(3,0) could be embedded in a single larger algebra (perhaps Cl(4,3)?), it could provide a genuinely new perspective on ER=EPR. Speculative but potentially high-payoff.

highest.rationale: Genuine structural insights beyond notation. These pages reveal mathematical structure that is invisible or obscured in the standard formalism. Each provides at least one result that is genuinely simpler or more illuminating in GA.

strong.rationale: Real structural insights with moderate computational benefit. The GA treatment makes key structures more explicit (rotors, gauge fields) but the core derivation logic is unchanged.

moderate.rationale: Primarily notational improvement with occasional structural insights. The underlying mathematics is equivalent but expressed differently. Each has 1-2 genuinely novel observations but these don't yet constitute independent results.

derivedUpgradePriority.rationale: All results are textbook STA/Clifford algebra (Hestenes 1966, Doran-Lasenby 2003). Every step can be cited to established literature. Minimal gap-closing needed.
Core GA results well-established; framework-specific connections (coherence loop, pseudoscalar triple role) need tighter formalization but are not speculative.

Division-algebras: highest framework-feedback potential but associativity relocation argument needs more mathematical development. Einstein-equations: mostly notation, could piggyback on gravity upgrade.

Product algebra formalism less established in GA literature. ER=EPR two-algebra problem is genuinely unsolved. These are the most speculative pages with highest risk but also highest potential for novel results.

frameworkFeedbackPotential.description: Pages whose GA insights could strengthen the main derivation chain

candidates.mechanism: Associativity relocation: O non-associativity → Cl(6) grade-3 elements. Could tighten the termination argument.
d = 3+1 minimality from I² = -1 requirement. Could strengthen the dimension-selection step.
CPT = I unification simplifies the proof structure. Could compress the target derivation.
GTG validates that the framework's causal-set-on-Minkowski picture is consistent with full GR. Conceptual support, not a new derivation.

openQuestions.question: Can Cl(1,3) and Cl(3,0)² be embedded in a single larger Clifford algebra to unify the ER=EPR correspondence?
Does the grade pattern (U(1)→SU(2)→SU(3) as grade 0→1→2) have a rigorous algebraic basis or is it numerological?
Can the bivector budget (monogamy) be made quantitative — does the number of bivectors in Cl(3,0) yield the exact monogamy inequality?
Does the rotor periodicity ↔ loop closure parallel extend to a formal homomorphism between the STA rotor group and the observer loop structure?

filteredOut.reason: More about probability theory (Gleason's theorem) than geometry
Core content is relational, not geometrical
Mostly coherence accounting with spacetime as background
